authors_text: Meiziniu Li,Jialun Cao,Yongqiang Tian,Tsz On Li,Ming Wen,Shing-Chi Cheung
title: COMET: Coverage-guided Model Generation For Deep Learning Library Testing
title_zh: COMET：用于深度学习库测试的覆盖率引导模型生成
comment: 34 pages, 12 figures
comments: 34 pages, 12 figures
abstract: Recent deep learning (DL) applications are mostly built on top of DL libraries. The quality assurance of these libraries is critical to the dependable deployment of DL applications. Techniques have been proposed to generate various DL models and apply them to test these libraries. However, their test effectiveness is constrained by the diversity of layer API calls in their generated DL models. Our study reveals that these techniques can cover at most 34.1% layer inputs, 25.9% layer parameter values, and 15.6% layer sequences. As a result, we find that many bugs arising from specific layer API calls (i.e., specific layer inputs, parameter values, or layer sequences) can be missed by existing techniques. Because of this limitation, we propose COMET to effectively generate DL models with diverse layer API calls for DL library testing. COMET: (1) designs a set of mutation operators and a coverage-based search algorithm to diversify layer inputs, layer parameter values, and layer sequences in DL models. (2) proposes a model synthesis method to boost the test efficiency without compromising the layer API call diversity. Our evaluation result shows that COMET outperforms baselines by covering twice as many layer inputs (69.7% vs. 34.1%), layer parameter values (50.2% vs. 25.9%), and layer sequences (39.0% vs. 15.6%) as those by the state-of-the-art. Moreover, COMET covers 3.4% more library branches than those by existing techniques. Finally, COMET detects 32 new bugs in the latest version of eight popular DL libraries, including TensorFlow and MXNet, with 21 of them confirmed by DL library developers and 7 of those confirmed bugs have been fixed by developers.
summary: Recent deep learning (DL) applications are mostly built on top of DL libraries. The quality assurance of these libraries is critical to the dependable deployment of DL applications. Techniques have been proposed to generate various DL models and apply them to test these libraries. However, their test effectiveness is constrained by the diversity of layer API calls in their generated DL models. Our study reveals that these techniques can cover at most 34.1% layer inputs, 25.9% layer parameter values, and 15.6% layer sequences. As a result, we find that many bugs arising from specific layer API calls (i.e., specific layer inputs, parameter values, or layer sequences) can be missed by existing techniques. Because of this limitation, we propose COMET to effectively generate DL models with diverse layer API calls for DL library testing. COMET: (1) designs a set of mutation operators and a coverage-based search algorithm to diversify layer inputs, layer parameter values, and layer sequences in DL models. (2) proposes a model synthesis method to boost the test efficiency without compromising the layer API call diversity. Our evaluation result shows that COMET outperforms baselines by covering twice as many layer inputs (69.7% vs. 34.1%), layer parameter values (50.2% vs. 25.9%), and layer sequences (39.0% vs. 15.6%) as those by the state-of-the-art. Moreover, COMET covers 3.4% more library branches than those by existing techniques. Finally, COMET detects 32 new bugs in the latest version of eight popular DL libraries, including TensorFlow and MXNet, with 21 of them confirmed by DL library developers and 7 of those confirmed bugs have been fixed by developers.
abstract_zh: 最近的深度学习 (DL) 应用程序大多构建在 DL 库之上。这些库的质量保证对于深度学习应用程序的可靠部署至关重要。人们已经提出了生成各种深度学习模型并应用它们来测试这些库的技术。然而，他们的测试有效性受到生成的深度学习模型中层 API 调用多样性的限制。我们的研究表明，这些技术最多可以覆盖 34.1% 的层输入、25.9% 的层参数值和 15.6% 的层序列。因此，我们发现现有技术可以忽略特定层 API 调用（即特定层输入、参数值或层序列）产生的许多错误。由于这一限制，我们建议 COMET 有效地生成具有不同层 API 调用的 DL 模型，以进行 DL 库测试。 COMET：（1）设计了一组变异算子和基于覆盖的搜索算法，以使深度学习模型中的层输入、层参数值和层序列多样化。 (2)提出了一种模型综合方法，在不影响层API调用多样性的情况下提高测试效率。我们的评估结果表明，COMET 的覆盖层输入（69.7% vs. 34.1%）、层参数值（50.2% vs. 25.9%）和层序列（39.0% vs. 15.6%）是现有技术的两倍，优于基线。此外，COMET 比现有技术覆盖的图书馆分支多了 3.4%。最后，COMET 在 TensorFlow 和 MXNet 等 8 个流行 DL 库的最新版本中检测到 32 个新错误，其中 21 个已得到 DL 库开发人员的确认，其中 7 个已确认的错误已被开发人员修复。

AI.tldr: 本文提出COMET方法，通过生成具有多样化的深度学习模型层API调用，提升对深度学习库测试的覆盖率和缺陷检测能力。
AI.motivation: 现有测试技术生成的深度学习模型在层输入、参数值和层序列的覆盖度不足，导致许多由特定API调用引发的缺陷被遗漏。
AI.method: 设计变异算子和基于覆盖的搜索算法以多样化模型结构，并提出模型合成方法提升测试效率。
AI.result: COMET覆盖的层输入（69.7%）、参数值（50.2%）和层序列（39.0%）是现有技术的2倍以上，检测到32个新缺陷，其中21个被开发者确认并修复7个。
AI.conclusion: COMET显著提升了深度学习库测试的覆盖度和缺陷检测能力，验证了其在实际应用中的有效性。

authors_text: Manuel Stoiber,Martin Sundermeyer,Wout Boerdijk,Rudolph Triebel
title: A Multi-body Tracking Framework - From Rigid Objects to Kinematic Structures
title_zh: 多体跟踪框架 - 从刚性物体到运动结构
comment: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
comments: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Kinematic structures are very common in the real world. They range from simple articulated objects to complex mechanical systems. However, despite their relevance, most model-based 3D tracking methods only consider rigid objects. To overcome this limitation, we propose a flexible framework that allows the extension of existing 6DoF algorithms to kinematic structures. Our approach focuses on methods that employ Newton-like optimization techniques, which are widely used in object tracking. The framework considers both tree-like and closed kinematic structures and allows a flexible configuration of joints and constraints. To project equations from individual rigid bodies to a multi-body system, Jacobians are used. For closed kinematic chains, a novel formulation that features Lagrange multipliers is developed. In a detailed mathematical proof, we show that our constraint formulation leads to an exact kinematic solution and converges in a single iteration. Based on the proposed framework, we extend ICG, which is a state-of-the-art rigid object tracking algorithm, to multi-body tracking. For the evaluation, we create a highly-realistic synthetic dataset that features a large number of sequences and various robots. Based on this dataset, we conduct a wide variety of experiments that demonstrate the excellent performance of the developed framework and our multi-body tracker.
summary: Kinematic structures are very common in the real world. They range from simple articulated objects to complex mechanical systems. However, despite their relevance, most model-based 3D tracking methods only consider rigid objects. To overcome this limitation, we propose a flexible framework that allows the extension of existing 6DoF algorithms to kinematic structures. Our approach focuses on methods that employ Newton-like optimization techniques, which are widely used in object tracking. The framework considers both tree-like and closed kinematic structures and allows a flexible configuration of joints and constraints. To project equations from individual rigid bodies to a multi-body system, Jacobians are used. For closed kinematic chains, a novel formulation that features Lagrange multipliers is developed. In a detailed mathematical proof, we show that our constraint formulation leads to an exact kinematic solution and converges in a single iteration. Based on the proposed framework, we extend ICG, which is a state-of-the-art rigid object tracking algorithm, to multi-body tracking. For the evaluation, we create a highly-realistic synthetic dataset that features a large number of sequences and various robots. Based on this dataset, we conduct a wide variety of experiments that demonstrate the excellent performance of the developed framework and our multi-body tracker.
abstract_zh: 运动结构在现实世界中非常常见。它们的范围从简单的铰接物体到复杂的机械系统。然而，尽管它们具有相关性，但大多数基于模型的 3D 跟踪方法仅考虑刚性对象。为了克服这个限制，我们提出了一个灵活的框架，允许将现有的 6DoF 算法扩展到运动结构。我们的方法侧重于采用类牛顿优化技术的方法，该技术广泛用于对象跟踪。该框架考虑了树状结构和封闭运动结构，并允许灵活配置关节和约束。为了将方程从单个刚体投影到多体系统，需要使用雅可比行列式。对于闭合运动链，开发了一种采用拉格朗日乘子的新颖公式。在详细的数学证明中，我们表明我们的约束公式可以得出精确的运动学解并在单次迭代中收敛。基于所提出的框架，我们将 ICG（一种最先进的刚性物体跟踪算法）扩展到多体跟踪。为了进行评估，我们创建了一个高度真实的合成数据集，其中包含大量序列和各种机器人。基于这个数据集，我们进行了各种各样的实验，证明了所开发的框架和我们的多体跟踪器的出色性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多体追踪框架，将基于模型的3D追踪方法从刚体扩展到树状和闭合运动学结构，通过牛顿优化技术及拉格朗日乘数法实现精确求解。
AI.motivation: 现有3D追踪方法仅适用于刚体，难以处理现实世界中普遍存在的复杂运动学结构（如机械臂、机器人等），需开发支持多体系统的灵活框架。
AI.method: 基于牛顿类优化算法，利用雅可比矩阵将单体方程扩展到多体系统；针对闭合链提出含拉格朗日乘数的新型约束公式，并扩展ICG算法实现多体追踪。
AI.result: 在高度逼真的合成数据集上验证了框架有效性，实验表明该方法对多种机器人序列具有优异追踪性能且单次迭代即可收敛。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架为运动学结构提供了精确且高效的多体追踪解决方案，为复杂机械系统的实时跟踪研究奠定基础。

To overcome this limitation, we propose a flexible framework that allows the extension of existing 6DoF algorithms to kinematic structures. The framework considers both tree-like and closed kinematic structures and allows a flexible configuration of joints and constraints. To project equations from individual rigid bodies to a multi-body system, Jacobians are used. For closed kinematic chains, a novel formulation that features Lagrange multipliers is developed. In a detailed mathematical proof, we show that our constraint formulation leads to an exact kinematic solution and converges in a single iteration. Based on the proposed framework, we extend ICG, which is a state-of-the-art rigid object tracking algorithm, to multi-body tracking. For the evaluation, we create a highly-realistic synthetic dataset that features a large number of sequences and various robots. authors_text: Qiangqiang Liu,Yukun Ren,Zhihua Zhu,Dai Li,Xiaojuan Ma,Quan Li
title: RankAxis: Towards a Systematic Combination of Projection and Ranking in Multi-Attribute Data Exploration
title_zh: RankAxis：在多属性数据探索中实现投影和排序的系统组合
comment: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics (Proc. IEEE VIS 2022)
comments: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics (Proc. IEEE VIS 2022)
abstract: Projection and ranking are frequently used analysis techniques in multi-attribute data exploration. Both families of techniques help analysts with tasks such as identifying similarities between observations and determining ordered subgroups, and have shown good performances in multi-attribute data exploration. However, they often exhibit problems such as distorted projection layouts, obscure semantic interpretations, and non-intuitive effects produced by selecting a subset of (weighted) attributes. Moreover, few studies have attempted to combine projection and ranking into the same exploration space to complement each other's strengths and weaknesses. For this reason, we propose RankAxis, a visual analytics system that systematically combines projection and ranking to facilitate the mutual interpretation of these two techniques and jointly support multi-attribute data exploration. A real-world case study, expert feedback, and a user study demonstrate the efficacy of RankAxis.
summary: Projection and ranking are frequently used analysis techniques in multi-attribute data exploration. Both families of techniques help analysts with tasks such as identifying similarities between observations and determining ordered subgroups, and have shown good performances in multi-attribute data exploration. However, they often exhibit problems such as distorted projection layouts, obscure semantic interpretations, and non-intuitive effects produced by selecting a subset of (weighted) attributes. Moreover, few studies have attempted to combine projection and ranking into the same exploration space to complement each other's strengths and weaknesses. For this reason, we propose RankAxis, a visual analytics system that systematically combines projection and ranking to facilitate the mutual interpretation of these two techniques and jointly support multi-attribute data exploration. A real-world case study, expert feedback, and a user study demonstrate the efficacy of RankAxis.
abstract_zh: 投影和排序是多属性数据探索中常用的分析技术。这两个技术系列都可以帮助分析师完成诸如识别观察之间的相似性和确定有序子组等任务，并且在多属性数据探索中表现出良好的性能。然而，它们经常表现出诸如扭曲的投影布局、模糊的语义解释以及通过选择（加权）属性子集产生的非直观效果等问题。此外，很少有研究尝试将预测和排名结合到同一探索空间中，以取长补短。为此，我们提出了RankAxis，一种可视化分析系统，系统地将投影和排名结合起来，以促进这两种技术的相互解释，共同支持多属性数据探索。真实案例研究、专家反馈和用户研究证明了 RankAxis 的功效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RankAxis系统，通过系统化结合投影与排序技术，提升多属性数据探索的效率与可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有投影和排序方法存在布局失真、语义模糊及属性权重选择不直观等问题，且鲜有研究尝试将两者结合以互补优势。
AI.method: 开发RankAxis可视化分析系统，通过联合投影与排序技术，在同一探索空间中实现两者的互解释与协同分析。
AI.result: 真实案例研究、专家反馈及用户实验验证了RankAxis在提升数据探索效果方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: RankAxis通过整合投影与排序技术，解决了传统方法的局限性，为多属性数据分析提供了更直观且系统的工具。

authors_text: Jaime Spencer,Chris Russell,Simon Hadfield,Richard Bowden
title: Deconstructing Self-Supervised Monocular Reconstruction: The Design Decisions that Matter
title_zh: 解构自监督单目重建：重要的设计决策
comment: https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark
comments: https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark
abstract: This paper presents an open and comprehensive framework to systematically evaluate state-of-the-art contributions to self-supervised monocular depth estimation. This includes pretraining, backbone, architectural design choices and loss functions. Many papers in this field claim novelty in either architecture design or loss formulation. However, simply updating the backbone of historical systems results in relative improvements of 25%, allowing them to outperform the majority of existing systems. A systematic evaluation of papers in this field was not straightforward. The need to compare like-with-like in previous papers means that longstanding errors in the evaluation protocol are ubiquitous in the field. It is likely that many papers were not only optimized for particular datasets, but also for errors in the data and evaluation criteria. To aid future research in this area, we release a modular codebase (https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark), allowing for easy evaluation of alternate design decisions against corrected data and evaluation criteria. We re-implement, validate and re-evaluate 16 state-of-the-art contributions and introduce a new dataset (SYNS-Patches) containing dense outdoor depth maps in a variety of both natural and urban scenes. This allows for the computation of informative metrics in complex regions such as depth boundaries.
summary: This paper presents an open and comprehensive framework to systematically evaluate state-of-the-art contributions to self-supervised monocular depth estimation. This includes pretraining, backbone, architectural design choices and loss functions. Many papers in this field claim novelty in either architecture design or loss formulation. However, simply updating the backbone of historical systems results in relative improvements of 25%, allowing them to outperform the majority of existing systems. A systematic evaluation of papers in this field was not straightforward. The need to compare like-with-like in previous papers means that longstanding errors in the evaluation protocol are ubiquitous in the field. It is likely that many papers were not only optimized for particular datasets, but also for errors in the data and evaluation criteria. To aid future research in this area, we release a modular codebase (https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark), allowing for easy evaluation of alternate design decisions against corrected data and evaluation criteria. We re-implement, validate and re-evaluate 16 state-of-the-art contributions and introduce a new dataset (SYNS-Patches) containing dense outdoor depth maps in a variety of both natural and urban scenes. This allows for the computation of informative metrics in complex regions such as depth boundaries.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个开放且全面的框架，用于系统地评估对自监督单目深度估计的最先进贡献。这包括预训练、主干、架构设计选择和损失函数。该领域的许多论文声称在架构设计或损失公式方面具有新颖性。然而，简单地更新历史系统的主干就会导致 25% 的相对改进，从而使它们的性能优于大多数现有系统。对该领域论文的系统评估并不简单。需要对以前的论文进行同类比较，这意味着评估协议中长期存在的错误在该领域普遍存在。许多论文可能不仅针对特定数据集进行了优化，而且还针对数据和评估标准中的错误进行了优化。为了帮助该领域的未来研究，我们发布了一个模块化代码库（https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark），允许根据更正的数据和评估标准轻松评估替代设计决策。我们重新实现、验证和重新评估 16 个最先进的贡献，并引入一个新数据集 (SYNS-Patches)，其中包含各种自然和城市场景中的密集户外深度图。这允许计算复杂区域（例如深度边界）中的信息量度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个开放框架，系统评估自监督单目深度估计领域的最新方法，分析其设计决策的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有研究常声称架构或损失函数的创新性，但简单更换主干网络即可获得25%的性能提升，且领域内评估协议存在长期错误，需统一基准以推动研究。
AI.method: 构建模块化代码库，重新实现16种前沿方法，引入SYNS-Patches数据集（含密集户外深度图），并修正评估标准与数据错误进行系统验证。
AI.result: 发现多数论文针对特定数据集及评估偏差优化，新数据集可有效计算复杂区域（如深度边界）的指标。
AI.conclusion: 未来研究需基于修正后的基准与数据集，避免因评估偏差导致的结论失真，推动领域方法的公平比较。

search_text: Deconstructing Self-Supervised Monocular Reconstruction: The Design Decisions that Matter 解构自监督单目重建：重要的设计决策 This paper presents an open and comprehensive framework to systematically evaluate state-of-the-art contributions to self-supervised monocular depth estimation. This includes pretraining, backbone, architectural design choices and loss functions. Many papers in this field claim novelty in either architecture design or loss formulation. However, simply updating the backbone of historical systems results in relative improvements of 25%, allowing them to outperform the majority of existing systems. A systematic evaluation of papers in this field was not straightforward. The need to compare like-with-like in previous papers means that longstanding errors in the evaluation protocol are ubiquitous in the field. It is likely that many papers were not only optimized for particular datasets, but also for errors in the data and evaluation criteria. To aid future research in this area, we release a modular codebase (https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark), allowing for easy evaluation of alternate design decisions against corrected data and evaluation criteria. We re-implement, validate and re-evaluate 16 state-of-the-art contributions and introduce a new dataset (SYNS-Patches) containing dense outdoor depth maps in a variety of both natural and urban scenes. This allows for the computation of informative metrics in complex regions such as depth boundaries. 本文提出了一个开放且全面的框架，用于系统地评估对自监督单目深度估计的最先进贡献。这包括预训练、主干、架构设计选择和损失函数。该领域的许多论文声称在架构设计或损失公式方面具有新颖性。然而，简单地更新历史系统的主干就会导致 25% 的相对改进，从而使它们的性能优于大多数现有系统。对该领域论文的系统评估并不简单。需要对以前的论文进行同类比较，这意味着评估协议中长期存在的错误在该领域普遍存在。许多论文可能不仅针对特定数据集进行了优化，而且还针对数据和评估标准中的错误进行了优化。为了帮助该领域的未来研究，我们发布了一个模块化代码库（https://github.com/jspenmar/monodepth_benchmark），允许根据更正的数据和评估标准轻松评估替代设计决策。我们重新实现、验证和重新评估 16 个最先进的贡献，并引入一个新数据集 (SYNS-Patches)，其中包含各种自然和城市场景中的密集户外深度图。这允许计算复杂区域（例如深度边界）中的信息量度。 本文提出一个开放框架，系统评估自监督单目深度估计领域的最新方法，分析其设计决策的影响。 Jaime Spencer,Chris Russell,Simon Hadfield,Richard Bowden cs.CV,cs.CG,cs.LG

authors_text: Ashish Kumar,R. K. Agrawal,Leve Joseph
title: IterMiUnet: A lightweight architecture for automatic blood vessel segmentation
title_zh: IterMiUnet：用于自动血管分割的轻量级架构
abstract: The automatic segmentation of blood vessels in fundus images can help analyze the condition of retinal vasculature, which is crucial for identifying various systemic diseases like hypertension, diabetes, etc. Despite the success of Deep Learning-based models in this segmentation task, most of them are heavily parametrized and thus have limited use in practical applications. This paper proposes IterMiUnet, a new lightweight convolution-based segmentation model that requires significantly fewer parameters and yet delivers performance similar to existing models. The model makes use of the excellent segmentation capabilities of Iternet architecture but overcomes its heavily parametrized nature by incorporating the encoder-decoder structure of MiUnet model within it. Thus, the new model reduces parameters without any compromise with the network's depth, which is necessary to learn abstract hierarchical concepts in deep models. This lightweight segmentation model speeds up training and inference time and is potentially helpful in the medical domain where data is scarce and, therefore, heavily parametrized models tend to overfit. The proposed model was evaluated on three publicly available datasets: DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE-DB1. Further cross-training and inter-rater variability evaluations have also been performed. The proposed model has a lot of potential to be utilized as a tool for the early diagnosis of many diseases.
summary: The automatic segmentation of blood vessels in fundus images can help analyze the condition of retinal vasculature, which is crucial for identifying various systemic diseases like hypertension, diabetes, etc. Despite the success of Deep Learning-based models in this segmentation task, most of them are heavily parametrized and thus have limited use in practical applications. This paper proposes IterMiUnet, a new lightweight convolution-based segmentation model that requires significantly fewer parameters and yet delivers performance similar to existing models. The model makes use of the excellent segmentation capabilities of Iternet architecture but overcomes its heavily parametrized nature by incorporating the encoder-decoder structure of MiUnet model within it. Thus, the new model reduces parameters without any compromise with the network's depth, which is necessary to learn abstract hierarchical concepts in deep models. This lightweight segmentation model speeds up training and inference time and is potentially helpful in the medical domain where data is scarce and, therefore, heavily parametrized models tend to overfit. The proposed model was evaluated on three publicly available datasets: DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE-DB1. Further cross-training and inter-rater variability evaluations have also been performed. The proposed model has a lot of potential to be utilized as a tool for the early diagnosis of many diseases.
abstract_zh: 眼底图像中血管的自动分割可以帮助分析视网膜脉管系统的状况，这对于识别高血压、糖尿病等各种系统性疾病至关重要。尽管基于深度学习的模型在此分割任务中取得了成功，但大多数模型参数化程度很高，因此在实际应用中的用途有限。本文提出了 IterMiUnet，一种新的基于卷积的轻量级分割模型，它需要的参数显着减少，但提供与现有模型相似的性能。该模型利用了 Iternet 架构出色的分段功能，但通过在其中合并 MiUnet 模型的编码器-解码器结构来克服其严重参数化的特性。因此，新模型在不影响网络深度的情况下减少了参数，这对于学习深度模型中的抽象层次概念是必要的。这种轻量级分割模型可以加快训练和推理时间，并且在数据稀缺的医疗领域可能会有所帮助，因此，高度参数化的模型往往会过度拟合。所提出的模型在三个公开可用的数据集上进行了评估：DRIVE、STARE 和 CHASE-DB1。还进行了进一步的交叉培训和评估者间的变异性评估。所提出的模型具有很大的潜力，可以用作许多疾病的早期诊断工具。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级的IterMiUnet模型，用于视网膜图像中血管的自动分割。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习血管分割模型参数过多，难以在实际应用中部署，尤其在数据稀缺的医疗领域易过拟合。
AI.method: 结合Iternet的优秀分割能力和MiUnet的编码器-解码器结构，设计轻量级卷积模型以减少参数量。
AI.result: 在DRIVE、STARE和CHASE-DB1三个数据集上验证，模型在保持性能的同时减少参数，并通过跨训练和评分者间差异评估验证有效性。
AI.conclusion: IterMiUnet在保持分割精度的前提下显著降低参数量，适用于医疗领域早期疾病诊断，具有较大应用潜力。

authors_text: Eyal Shnarch,Alon Halfon,Ariel Gera,Marina Danilevsky,Yannis Katsis,Leshem Choshen,Martin Santillan Cooper,Dina Epelboim,Zheng Zhang,Dakuo Wang,Lucy Yip,Liat Ein-Dor,Lena Dankin,Ilya Shnayderman,Ranit Aharonov,Yunyao Li,Naftali Liberman,Philip Levin Slesarev,Gwilym Newton,Shila Ofek-Koifman,Noam Slonim,Yoav Katz
title: Label Sleuth: From Unlabeled Text to a Classifier in a Few Hours
title_zh: 标签侦探：在几个小时内从无标签文本到分类器
comment: 7 pages, 2 figures To be published at EMNLP 2022
comments: 7 pages, 2 figures To be published at EMNLP 2022
abstract: Text classification can be useful in many real-world scenarios, saving a lot of time for end users. However, building a custom classifier typically requires coding skills and ML knowledge, which poses a significant barrier for many potential users. To lift this barrier, we introduce Label Sleuth, a free open source system for labeling and creating text classifiers. This system is unique for (a) being a no-code system, making NLP accessible to non-experts, (b) guiding users through the entire labeling process until they obtain a custom classifier, making the process efficient -- from cold start to classifier in a few hours, and (c) being open for configuration and extension by developers. By open sourcing Label Sleuth we hope to build a community of users and developers that will broaden the utilization of NLP models.
summary: Text classification can be useful in many real-world scenarios, saving a lot of time for end users. However, building a custom classifier typically requires coding skills and ML knowledge, which poses a significant barrier for many potential users. To lift this barrier, we introduce Label Sleuth, a free open source system for labeling and creating text classifiers. This system is unique for (a) being a no-code system, making NLP accessible to non-experts, (b) guiding users through the entire labeling process until they obtain a custom classifier, making the process efficient -- from cold start to classifier in a few hours, and (c) being open for configuration and extension by developers. By open sourcing Label Sleuth we hope to build a community of users and developers that will broaden the utilization of NLP models.
abstract_zh: 文本分类在许多现实场景中都很有用，可以为最终用户节省大量时间。然而，构建自定义分类器通常需要编码技能和机器学习知识，这对许多潜在用户构成了重大障碍。为了消除这一障碍，我们引入了 Label Sleuth，这是一个用于标记和创建文本分类器的免费开源系统。该系统的独特之处在于（a）是一个无代码系统，使非专家也可以使用 NLP；（b）指导用户完成整个标记过程，直到他们获得自定义分类器，从而使该过程高效——从冷启动到分类器只需几个小时；（c）开放供开发人员配置和扩展。通过开源 Label Sleuth，我们希望建立一个用户和开发人员社区，以扩大 NLP 模型的利用率。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一种名为Label Sleuth的无代码系统，旨在帮助非专家用户快速从无标签文本创建文本分类器。
AI.motivation: 传统文本分类需要编程和机器学习知识，导致许多潜在用户无法使用。为此，需开发一个低门槛、高效且可扩展的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过构建一个开源的无代码平台，引导用户完成从数据标注到模型训练的全流程，并支持开发者自定义扩展。
AI.result: 系统实现了从冷启动到生成分类器仅需数小时，降低了NLP应用的门槛并促进了社区协作。
AI.conclusion: 开源社区的参与将推动NLP技术更广泛的应用和持续改进。

By open sourcing Label Sleuth we hope to build a community of users and developers that will broaden the utilization of NLP models. 文本分类在许多现实场景中都很有用，可以为最终用户节省大量时间。然而，构建自定义分类器通常需要编码技能和机器学习知识，这对许多潜在用户构成了重大障碍。为了消除这一障碍，我们引入了 Label Sleuth，这是一个用于标记和创建文本分类器的免费开源系统。该系统的独特之处在于（a）是一个无代码系统，使非专家也可以使用 NLP；（b）指导用户完成整个标记过程，直到他们获得自定义分类器，从而使该过程高效——从冷启动到分类器只需几个小时；（c）开放供开发人员配置和扩展。通过开源 Label Sleuth，我们希望建立一个用户和开发人员社区，以扩大 NLP 模型的利用率。 本文介绍了一种名为Label Sleuth的无代码系统，旨在帮助非专家用户快速从无标签文本创建文本分类器。 Eyal Shnarch,Alon Halfon,Ariel Gera,Marina Danilevsky,Yannis Katsis,Leshem Choshen,Martin Santillan Cooper,Dina Epelboim,Zheng Zhang,Dakuo Wang,Lucy Yip,Liat Ein-Dor,Lena Dankin,Ilya Shnayderman,Ranit Aharonov,Yunyao Li,Naftali Liberman,Philip Levin Slesarev,Gwilym Newton,Shila Ofek-Koifman,Noam Slonim,Yoav Katz cs.CL,cs.HC

authors_text: Diego S. D'Antonio,David Saldaña
title: Folding Knots Using a Team of Aerial Robots
title_zh: 使用空中机器人团队折叠结
comment: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, IROS 2022, Kyoto, Japan, Oct 23 - Oct. 27, 2022
comments: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, IROS 2022, Kyoto, Japan, Oct 23 - Oct. 27, 2022
abstract: From ancient times, humans have been using cables and ropes to tie, carry, and manipulate objects by folding knots. However, automating knot folding is challenging because it requires dexterity to move a cable over and under itself. In this paper, we propose a method to fold knots in midair using a team of aerial vehicles. We take advantage of the fact that vehicles are able to fly in between cable segments without any re-grasping. So the team grasps the cable from the floor, and releases it once the knot is folded. Based on a composition of catenary curves, we simplify the complexity of dealing with an infinite-dimensional configuration space of the cable, and formally propose a new knot representation. Such representation allows us to design a trajectory that can be used to fold knots using a leader-follower approach. We show that our method works for different types of knots in simulations. Additionally, we show that our solution is also computationally efficient and can be executed in real-time.
summary: From ancient times, humans have been using cables and ropes to tie, carry, and manipulate objects by folding knots. However, automating knot folding is challenging because it requires dexterity to move a cable over and under itself. In this paper, we propose a method to fold knots in midair using a team of aerial vehicles. We take advantage of the fact that vehicles are able to fly in between cable segments without any re-grasping. So the team grasps the cable from the floor, and releases it once the knot is folded. Based on a composition of catenary curves, we simplify the complexity of dealing with an infinite-dimensional configuration space of the cable, and formally propose a new knot representation. Such representation allows us to design a trajectory that can be used to fold knots using a leader-follower approach. We show that our method works for different types of knots in simulations. Additionally, we show that our solution is also computationally efficient and can be executed in real-time.
abstract_zh: 从远古时代开始，人类就一直使用缆绳和绳索通过折叠结来捆绑、携带和操纵物体。然而，自动打结折叠具有挑战性，因为它需要灵巧地在其自身上方和下方移动电缆。在本文中，我们提出了一种使用一组飞行器在半空中折叠结的方法。我们利用了这样一个事实，即车辆能够在电缆段之间飞行而无需重新抓取。因此，团队从地板上抓住电缆，并在结折叠后将其释放。基于悬链线的组合，我们简化了处理无限维缆索配置空间的复杂性，并正式提出了一种新的结表示。这种表示方式使我们能够设计一条轨迹，可用于使用领导者-跟随者方法来折叠结。我们证明我们的方法适用于模拟中不同类型的结。此外，我们还表明我们的解决方案计算效率也很高，并且可以实时执行。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用无人机团队在空中折叠绳结的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统绳结折叠需要高灵巧度操作，自动化难度大，而无人机可在空中灵活移动，无需重新抓取绳索。
AI.method: 基于悬链线曲线组合简化绳索配置空间，提出新的结的表示方法，并采用领导者-跟随者轨迹规划策略。
AI.result: 方法在仿真中成功折叠多种绳结，且计算效率高，可实时执行。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为自动化绳结折叠提供了有效解决方案，展示了无人机团队在复杂操作中的潜力。

authors_text: Barbara Berti,Andrea Esuli,Fabrizio Sebastiani
title: Unravelling Interlanguage Facts via Explainable Machine Learning
title_zh: 通过可解释的机器学习揭示中介语事实
abstract: Native language identification (NLI) is the task of training (via supervised machine learning) a classifier that guesses the native language of the author of a text. This task has been extensively researched in the last decade, and the performance of NLI systems has steadily improved over the years. We focus on a different facet of the NLI task, i.e., that of analysing the internals of an NLI classifier trained by an \emph{explainable} machine learning algorithm, in order to obtain explanations of its classification decisions, with the ultimate goal of gaining insight into which linguistic phenomena ``give a speaker's native language away''. We use this perspective in order to tackle both NLI and a (much less researched) companion task, i.e., guessing whether a text has been written by a native or a non-native speaker. Using three datasets of different provenance (two datasets of English learners' essays and a dataset of social media posts), we investigate which kind of linguistic traits (lexical, morphological, syntactic, and statistical) are most effective for solving our two tasks, namely, are most indicative of a speaker's L1. We also present two case studies, one on Spanish and one on Italian learners of English, in which we analyse individual linguistic traits that the classifiers have singled out as most important for spotting these L1s. Overall, our study shows that the use of explainable machine learning can be a valuable tool for th

Overall, our study shows that the use of explainable machine learning can be a valuable tool for th
abstract_zh: 母语识别 (NLI) 是训练（通过监督机器学习）猜测文本作者母语的分类器的任务。这项任务在过去十年中得到了广泛的研究，并且 NLI 系统的性能多年来一直在稳步提高。我们关注 NLI 任务的另一个方面，即分析由 \emph{explainable} 机器学习算法训练的 NLI 分类器的内部结构，以获得其分类决策的解释，最终目标是深入了解哪些语言现象“泄露了说话者的母语”。我们使用这个视角来解决 NLI 和（研究较少的）伴随任务，即猜测文本是由母语人士还是非母语人士撰写的。使用三个不同来源的数据集（两个英语学习者论文数据集和一个社交媒体帖子数据集），我们研究哪种语言特征（词汇、形态、句法和统计）对于解决我们的两个任务最有效，即最能反映说话者的母语水平。我们还提出了两个案例研究，一个关于西班牙语，一个关于意大利语英语学习者，在其中我们分析了分类器认为对于识别这些 L1 最重要的个人语言特征。总的来说，我们的研究表明，使用可解释的机器学习可以成为一个有价值的工具。

AI.tldr: 本文通过可解释机器学习分析非母语者语言特征，揭示其母语对二语使用的影响。
AI.motivation: 传统母语识别研究侧重系统性能，而本文旨在通过可解释性方法挖掘语言特征与母语关联的内在规律。
AI.method: 采用可解释机器学习算法，结合三类数据集（作文与社交媒体文本），分析词汇、形态、句法等语言特征，并开展西班牙语与意大利语学习者的案例研究。
AI.result: 发现特定语言特征（如词汇使用模式、语法结构）能有效指示母语背景，案例研究验证了不同母语对二语影响的差异性。
AI.conclusion: 可解释机器学习为揭示中介语现象提供了有效工具，有助于理解语言迁移机制。

authors_text: Tudor Jianu,Baoru Huang,Mohamed E. M. K. Abdelaziz,Minh Nhat Vu,Sebastiano Fichera,Chun-Yi Lee,Pierre Berthet-Rayne,Ferdinando Rodriguez y Baena,Anh Nguyen
title: CathSim: An Open-source Simulator for Endovascular Intervention
title_zh: CathSim：用于血管内介入的开源模拟器
abstract: Autonomous robots in endovascular operations have the potential to navigate circulatory systems safely and reliably while decreasing the susceptibility to human errors. However, there are numerous challenges involved with the process of training such robots, such as long training duration and safety issues arising from the interaction between the catheter and the aorta. Recently, endovascular simulators have been employed for medical training but generally do not conform to autonomous catheterization. Furthermore, most current simulators are closed-source, which hinders the collaborative development of safe and reliable autonomous systems. In this work, we introduce CathSim, an open-source simulation environment that accelerates the development of machine learning algorithms for autonomous endovascular navigation. We first simulate the high-fidelity catheter and aorta with a state-of-the-art endovascular robot. We then provide the capability of real-time force sensing between the catheter and the aorta in simulation. Furthermore, we validate our simulator by conducting two different catheterization tasks using two popular reinforcement learning algorithms. The experimental results show that our open-source simulator can mimic the behaviour of real-world endovascular robots and facilitate the development of different autonomous catheterization tasks. Our simulator is publicly available at https://github.com/robotvisionlabs/cathsim.
summary: Autonomous robots in endovascular operations have the potential to navigate circulatory systems safely and reliably while decreasing the susceptibility to human errors. However, there are numerous challenges involved with the process of training such robots, such as long training duration and safety issues arising from the interaction between the catheter and the aorta. Recently, endovascular simulators have been employed for medical training but generally do not conform to autonomous catheterization. Furthermore, most current simulators are closed-source, which hinders the collaborative development of safe and reliable autonomous systems. In this work, we introduce CathSim, an open-source simulation environment that accelerates the development of machine learning algorithms for autonomous endovascular navigation. We first simulate the high-fidelity catheter and aorta with a state-of-the-art endovascular robot. We then provide the capability of real-time force sensing between the catheter and the aorta in simulation. Furthermore, we validate our simulator by conducting two different catheterization tasks using two popular reinforcement learning algorithms. The experimental results show that our open-source simulator can mimic the behaviour of real-world endovascular robots and facilitate the development of different autonomous catheterization tasks. Our simulator is publicly available at https://github.com/robotvisionlabs/cathsim.
abstract_zh: 血管内手术中的自主机器人有可能安全可靠地导航循环系统，同时降低人为错误的可能性。然而，训练此类机器人的过程存在许多挑战，例如训练时间长以及导管与主动脉之间相互作用产生的安全问题。最近，血管内模拟器已用于医疗培训，但通常不符合自主导管插入术。此外，当前大多数模拟器都是闭源的，这阻碍了安全可靠的自主系统的协作开发。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 CathSim，这是一个开源模拟环境，可加速自主血管内导航机器学习算法的开发。我们首先用最先进的血管内机器人模拟高保真导管和主动脉。然后，我们在模拟中提供导管和主动脉之间的实时力传感能力。此外，我们通过使用两种流行的强化学习算法执行两种不同的导管插入任务来验证我们的模拟器。实验结果表明，我们的开源模拟器可以模仿现实世界血管内机器人的行为，并促进不同自主导管插入任务的开发。我们的模拟器可在 https://github.com/robotvisionlabs/cathsim 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 开发CathSim开源模拟器以加速自主血管内手术机器人的机器学习算法研发。
AI.motivation: 现有血管内手术模拟器闭源且不支持自主导管操作，训练耗时且存在安全风险。
AI.method: 构建高保真导管与主动脉物理模型，实现实时力反馈，并通过强化学习算法验证模拟器有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明模拟器能准确复现真实机器人行为，支持多种自主导管任务的算法开发。
AI.conclusion: CathSim作为开源工具可推动安全可靠的自主血管手术系统研发。

search_text: CathSim: An Open-source Simulator for Endovascular Intervention CathSim：用于血管内介入的开源模拟器 Autonomous robots in endovascular operations have the potential to navigate circulatory systems safely and reliably while decreasing the susceptibility to human errors. However, there are numerous challenges involved with the process of training such robots, such as long training duration and safety issues arising from the interaction between the catheter and the aorta. Recently, endovascular simulators have been employed for medical training but generally do not conform to autonomous catheterization. Furthermore, most current simulators are closed-source, which hinders the collaborative development of safe and reliable autonomous systems. In this work, we introduce CathSim, an open-source simulation environment that accelerates the development of machine learning algorithms for autonomous endovascular navigation. We first simulate the high-fidelity catheter and aorta with a state-of-the-art endovascular robot. We then provide the capability of real-time force sensing between the catheter and the aorta in simulation. Furthermore, we validate our simulator by conducting two different catheterization tasks using two popular reinforcement learning algorithms. The experimental results show that our open-source simulator can mimic the behaviour of real-world endovascular robots and facilitate the development of different autonomous catheterization tasks. Our simulator is publicly available at https://github.com/robotvisionlabs/cathsim. 血管内手术中的自主机器人有可能安全可靠地导航循环系统，同时降低人为错误的可能性。然而，训练此类机器人的过程存在许多挑战，例如训练时间长以及导管与主动脉之间相互作用产生的安全问题。最近，血管内模拟器已用于医疗培训，但通常不符合自主导管插入术。此外，当前大多数模拟器都是闭源的，这阻碍了安全可靠的自主系统的协作开发。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 CathSim，这是一个开源模拟环境，可加速自主血管内导航机器学习算法的开发。我们首先用最先进的血管内机器人模拟高保真导管和主动脉。然后，我们在模拟中提供导管和主动脉之间的实时力传感能力。此外，我们通过使用两种流行的强化学习算法执行两种不同的导管插入任务来验证我们的模拟器。实验结果表明，我们的开源模拟器可以模仿现实世界血管内机器人的行为，并促进不同自主导管插入任务的开发。我们的模拟器可在 https://github.com/robotvisionlabs/cathsim 上公开获取。 开发CathSim开源模拟器以加速自主血管内手术机器人的机器学习算法研发。 Tudor Jianu,Baoru Huang,Mohamed E. authors_text: Rui-Yang Ju,Jen-Shiun Chiang,Chih-Chia Chen,Yu-Shian Lin
title: Connection Reduction of DenseNet for Image Recognition
title_zh: 用于图像识别的 DenseNet 连接减少
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) increase depth by stacking convolutional layers, and deeper network models perform better in image recognition. Empirical research shows that simply stacking convolutional layers does not make the network train better, and skip connection (residual learning) can improve network model performance. For the image classification task, models with global densely connected architectures perform well in large datasets like ImageNet, but are not suitable for small datasets such as CIFAR-10 and SVHN. Different from dense connections, we propose two new algorithms to connect layers. Baseline is a densely connected network, and the networks connected by the two new algorithms are named ShortNet1 and ShortNet2 respectively. The experimental results of image classification on CIFAR-10 and SVHN show that ShortNet1 has a 5% lower test error rate and 25% faster inference time than Baseline. ShortNet2 speeds up inference time by 40% with less loss in test accuracy. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/RuiyangJu/Connection_Reduction.
summary: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) increase depth by stacking convolutional layers, and deeper network models perform better in image recognition. Empirical research shows that simply stacking convolutional layers does not make the network train better, and skip connection (residual learning) can improve network model performance. For the image classification task, models with global densely connected architectures perform well in large datasets like ImageNet, but are not suitable for small datasets such as CIFAR-10 and SVHN. Different from dense connections, we propose two new algorithms to connect layers. Baseline is a densely connected network, and the networks connected by the two new algorithms are named ShortNet1 and ShortNet2 respectively. The experimental results of image classification on CIFAR-10 and SVHN show that ShortNet1 has a 5% lower test error rate and 25% faster inference time than Baseline. ShortNet2 speeds up inference time by 40% with less loss in test accuracy. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/RuiyangJu/Connection_Reduction.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）通过堆叠卷积层来增加深度，更深的网络模型在图像识别中表现更好。实证研究表明，简单地堆叠卷积层并不能让网络训练得更好，而跳过连接（残差学习）可以提高网络模型性能。对于图像分类任务，具有全局密集连接架构的模型在 ImageNet 等大型数据集上表现良好，但不适合 CIFAR-10 和 SVHN 等小型数据集。与密集连接不同，我们提出了两种新的层连接算法。 Baseline是一个密集连接的网络，两种新算法连接的网络分别命名为ShortNet1和ShortNet2。在 CIFAR-10 和 SVHN 上的图像分类实验结果表明，ShortNet1 的测试错误率比 Baseline 低 5%，推理时间快 25%。 ShortNet2 将推理时间加快了 40%，同时测试精度损失更小。代码和预训练模型可在 https://github.com/RuiyangJu/Connection_Reduction 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过减少DenseNet的连接数量，优化其在小数据集上的图像识别性能。
AI.motivation: 现有密集连接网络在小数据集上表现不佳且计算复杂度高，需寻找更高效的连接方式。
AI.method: 提出两种新的网络连接算法（ShortNet1和ShortNet2），通过减少层间连接数量来降低计算复杂度。
AI.result: 实验显示，ShortNet1在CIFAR-10和SVHN上测试错误率降低5%，推理速度提升25%；ShortNet2推理速度提升40%且准确率损失较小。
AI.conclusion: 两种新算法有效平衡了模型效率与性能，在小数据集上优于传统DenseNet。

authors_text: Mikhail Usvyatsov,Rafael Ballester-Rippoll,Lina Bashaeva,Konrad Schindler,Gonzalo Ferrer,Ivan Oseledets
title: T4DT: Tensorizing Time for Learning Temporal 3D Visual Data
title_zh: T4DT：学习时态 3D 视觉数据的张量化时间
abstract: Unlike 2D raster images, there is no single dominant representation for 3D visual data processing. Different formats like point clouds, meshes, or implicit functions each have their strengths and weaknesses. Still, grid representations such as signed distance functions have attractive properties also in 3D. In particular, they offer constant-time random access and are eminently suitable for modern machine learning. Unfortunately, the storage size of a grid grows exponentially with its dimension. Hence they often exceed memory limits even at moderate resolution. This work proposes using low-rank tensor formats, including the Tucker, tensor train, and quantics tensor train decompositions, to compress time-varying 3D data. Our method iteratively computes, voxelizes, and compresses each frame's truncated signed distance function and applies tensor rank truncation to condense all frames into a single, compressed tensor that represents the entire 4D scene. We show that low-rank tensor compression is extremely compact to store and query time-varying signed distance functions. It significantly reduces the memory footprint of 4D scenes while remarkably preserving their geometric quality. Unlike existing, iterative learning-based approaches like DeepSDF and NeRF, our method uses a closed-form algorithm with theoretical guarantees.
summary: Unlike 2D raster images, there is no single dominant representation for 3D visual data processing. Different formats like point clouds, meshes, or implicit functions each have their strengths and weaknesses. Still, grid representations such as signed distance functions have attractive properties also in 3D. In particular, they offer constant-time random access and are eminently suitable for modern machine learning. Unfortunately, the storage size of a grid grows exponentially with its dimension. Hence they often exceed memory limits even at moderate resolution. This work proposes using low-rank tensor formats, including the Tucker, tensor train, and quantics tensor train decompositions, to compress time-varying 3D data. Our method iteratively computes, voxelizes, and compresses each frame's truncated signed distance function and applies tensor rank truncation to condense all frames into a single, compressed tensor that represents the entire 4D scene. We show that low-rank tensor compression is extremely compact to store and query time-varying signed distance functions. It significantly reduces the memory footprint of 4D scenes while remarkably preserving their geometric quality. Unlike existing, iterative learning-based approaches like DeepSDF and NeRF, our method uses a closed-form algorithm with theoretical guarantees.
abstract_zh: 与 2D 光栅图像不同，3D 视觉数据处理没有单一的主导表示。点云、网格或隐函数等不同格式各有其优点和缺点。尽管如此，诸如带符号距离函数之类的网格表示在 3D 中也具有有吸引力的特性。特别是，它们提供恒定时间随机访问，非常适合现代机器学习。不幸的是，网格的存储大小随着其尺寸呈指数增长。因此，即使在中等分辨率下，它们也经常超出内存限制。这项工作建议使用低秩张量格式（包括 Tucker、张量序列和量子张量序列分解）来压缩时变 3D 数据。我们的方法迭代计算、体素化和压缩每个帧的截断符号距离函数，并应用张量秩截断将所有帧压缩为表示整个 4D 场景的单个压缩张量。我们证明低秩张量压缩对于存储和查询时变有符号距离函数来说非常紧凑。它显着减少了 4D 场景的内存占用，同时显着保留了其几何质量。与 DeepSDF 和 NeRF 等现有的基于迭代学习的方法不同，我们的方法使用具有理论保证的封闭式算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出T4DT方法，利用低秩张量格式压缩时变3D数据，以解决网格表示在存储和查询上的内存限制问题。
AI.motivation: 3D数据的网格表示（如符号距离函数）虽具常数时间随机访问优势，但存储需求随维度指数增长，导致内存超出限制。现有方法如DeepSDF和NeRF依赖迭代学习且缺乏理论保证。
AI.method: 采用Tucker、张量链和量化张量链分解，对每帧截断符号距离函数进行体素化压缩，并通过张量秩截断将所有帧整合为单个压缩张量，实现4D场景的紧凑表示。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法显著降低4D场景的内存占用，同时保持几何质量，优于现有迭代学习方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于闭式算法的低秩张量压缩方法，在理论保证下有效解决了时变3D数据的存储与查询问题，为4D场景建模提供高效方案。

search_text: T4DT: Tensorizing Time for Learning Temporal 3D Visual Data T4DT：学习时态 3D 视觉数据的张量化时间 Unlike 2D raster images, there is no single dominant representation for 3D visual data processing. Different formats like point clouds, meshes, or implicit functions each have their strengths and weaknesses. Still, grid representations such as signed distance functions have attractive properties also in 3D. In particular, they offer constant-time random access and are eminently suitable for modern machine learning. Unfortunately, the storage size of a grid grows exponentially with its dimension. Hence they often exceed memory limits even at moderate resolution. This work proposes using low-rank tensor formats, including the Tucker, tensor train, and quantics tensor train decompositions, to compress time-varying 3D data. Our method iteratively computes, voxelizes, and compresses each frame's truncated signed distance function and applies tensor rank truncation to condense all frames into a single, compressed tensor that represents the entire 4D scene. We show that low-rank tensor compression is extremely compact to store and query time-varying signed distance functions. It significantly reduces the memory footprint of 4D scenes while remarkably preserving their geometric quality. Unlike existing, iterative learning-based approaches like DeepSDF and NeRF, our method uses a closed-form algorithm with theoretical guarantees. 与 2D 光栅图像不同，3D 视觉数据处理没有单一的主导表示。点云、网格或隐函数等不同格式各有其优点和缺点。尽管如此，诸如带符号距离函数之类的网格表示在 3D 中也具有有吸引力的特性。特别是，它们提供恒定时间随机访问，非常适合现代机器学习。不幸的是，网格的存储大小随着其尺寸呈指数增长。因此，即使在中等分辨率下，它们也经常超出内存限制。这项工作建议使用低秩张量格式（包括 Tucker、张量序列和量子张量序列分解）来压缩时变 3D 数据。我们的方法迭代计算、体素化和压缩每个帧的截断符号距离函数，并应用张量秩截断将所有帧压缩为表示整个 4D 场景的单个压缩张量。我们证明低秩张量压缩对于存储和查询时变有符号距离函数来说非常紧凑。它显着减少了 4D 场景的内存占用，同时显着保留了其几何质量。与 DeepSDF 和 NeRF 等现有的基于迭代学习的方法不同，我们的方法使用具有理论保证的封闭式算法。 本文提出T4DT方法，利用低秩张量格式压缩时变3D数据，以解决网格表示在存储和查询上的内存限制问题。 Mikhail Usvyatsov,Rafael Ballester-Rippoll,Lina Bashaeva,Konrad Schindler,Gonzalo Ferrer,Ivan Oseledets cs.CV

authors_text: Marco Zaffalon,Alessandro Antonucci,Rafael Cabañas,David Huber,Dario Azzimonti
title: Bounding Counterfactuals under Selection Bias
title_zh: 选择偏差下的边界反事实
comment: Eleventh International Conference on Probabilistic Graphical Models (PGM 2022)
comments: Eleventh International Conference on Probabilistic Graphical Models (PGM 2022)
abstract: Causal analysis may be affected by selection bias, which is defined as the systematic exclusion of data from a certain subpopulation. Previous work in this area focused on the derivation of identifiability conditions. We propose instead a first algorithm to address both identifiable and unidentifiable queries. We prove that, in spite of the missingness induced by the selection bias, the likelihood of the available data is unimodal. This enables us to use the causal expectation-maximisation scheme to obtain the values of causal queries in the identifiable case, and to compute bounds otherwise. Experiments demonstrate the approach to be practically viable. Theoretical convergence characterisations are provided.
summary: Causal analysis may be affected by selection bias, which is defined as the systematic exclusion of data from a certain subpopulation. Previous work in this area focused on the derivation of identifiability conditions. We propose instead a first algorithm to address both identifiable and unidentifiable queries. We prove that, in spite of the missingness induced by the selection bias, the likelihood of the available data is unimodal. This enables us to use the causal expectation-maximisation scheme to obtain the values of causal queries in the identifiable case, and to compute bounds otherwise. Experiments demonstrate the approach to be practically viable. Theoretical convergence characterisations are provided.
abstract_zh: 因果分析可能会受到选择偏差的影响，选择偏差被定义为系统地排除某个亚群体的数据。该领域之前的工作主要集中在可识别性条件的推导上。相反，我们提出了第一种算法来解决可识别和不可识别的查询。我们证明，尽管选择偏差导致了缺失，但可用数据的可能性是单峰的。这使我们能够使用因果期望最大化方案来获取可识别情况下因果查询的值，并在其他情况下计算界限。实验证明该方法实际上是可行的。提供了理论收敛特性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种算法，用于在存在选择偏差的情况下处理可识别和不可识别的反事实因果查询。
AI.motivation: 传统因果分析方法在处理选择偏差时仅关注可识别性条件，无法有效处理不可识别的查询，需更通用的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过证明可用数据似然函数的单峰性，结合因果期望最大化（EM）算法，实现对可识别查询的估计及不可识别查询的界限计算。
AI.result: 实验验证了方法的实用性，理论分析证明了算法的收敛性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为存在选择偏差的因果推理提供了统一框架，兼顾可识别与不可识别场景，具有理论与实践价值。

authors_text: Jian-Hui Chen,Cheng-Lin Liu,Zuoren Wang
title: Biologically Plausible Training of Deep Neural Networks Using a Top-down Credit Assignment Network
title_zh: 使用自上而下的信用分配网络对深度神经网络进行生物学上合理的训练
abstract: Despite the widespread adoption of Backpropagation algorithm-based Deep Neural Networks, the biological infeasibility of the BP algorithm could potentially limit the evolution of new DNN models. To find a biologically plausible algorithm to replace BP, we focus on the top-down mechanism inherent in the biological brain. Although top-down connections in the biological brain play crucial roles in high-level cognitive functions, their application to neural network learning remains unclear. This study proposes a two-level training framework designed to train a bottom-up network using a Top-Down Credit Assignment Network (TDCA-network). The TDCA-network serves as a substitute for the conventional loss function and the back-propagation algorithm, widely used in neural network training. We further introduce a brain-inspired credit diffusion mechanism, significantly reducing the TDCA-network's parameter complexity, thereby greatly accelerating training without compromising the network's performance.Our experiments involving non-convex function optimization, supervised learning, and reinforcement learning reveal that a well-trained TDCA-network outperforms back-propagation across various settings. The visualization of the update trajectories in the loss landscape indicates the TDCA-network's ability to bypass local minima where BP-based trajectories typically become trapped. The TDCA-network also excels in multi-task optimization, demonstrating robust generalizability across different datasets in supervised learning and unseen task settings in reinforcement learning. Moreover, the results indicate that the TDCA-network holds promising potential to train neural networks across diverse architectures.
summary: Despite the widespread adoption of Backpropagation algorithm-based Deep Neural Networks, the biological infeasibility of the BP algorithm could potentially limit the evolution of new DNN models. To find a biologically plausible algorithm to replace BP, we focus on the top-down mechanism inherent in the biological brain. Although top-down connections in the biological brain play crucial roles in high-level cognitive functions, their application to neural network learning remains unclear. This study proposes a two-level training framework designed to train a bottom-up network using a Top-Down Credit Assignment Network (TDCA-network). The TDCA-network serves as a substitute for the conventional loss function and the back-propagation algorithm, widely used in neural network training. We further introduce a brain-inspired credit diffusion mechanism, significantly reducing the TDCA-network's parameter complexity, thereby greatly accelerating training without compromising the network's performance.Our experiments involving non-convex function optimization, supervised learning, and reinforcement learning reveal that a well-trained TDCA-network outperforms back-propagation across various settings. The visualization of the update trajectories in the loss landscape indicates the TDCA-network's ability to bypass local minima where BP-based trajectories typically become trapped. The TDCA-network also excels in multi-task optimization, demonstrating robust generalizability across different datasets in supervised learning and unseen task settings in reinforcement learning. Moreover, the results indicate that the TDCA-network holds promising potential to train neural networks across diverse architectures.
abstract_zh: 尽管基于反向传播算法的深度神经网络得到了广泛采用，但 BP 算法的生物学不可行性可能会限制新 DNN 模型的发展。为了找到一种生物学上合理的算法来替代BP，我们关注生物大脑固有的自上而下的机制。尽管生物大脑中自上而下的连接在高级认知功能中发挥着至关重要的作用，但它们在神经网络学习中的应用仍不清楚。本研究提出了一个两级训练框架，旨在使用自上而下的信用分配网络（TDCA-网络）训练自下而上的网络。 TDCA网络作为传统损失函数和反向传播算法的替代品，广泛应用于神经网络训练。我们进一步引入了受大脑启发的信用扩散机制，显着降低了 TDCA 网络的参数复杂性，从而在不影响网络性能的情况下大大加快了训练速度。我们涉及非凸函数优化、监督学习和强化学习的实验表明，训练有素的 TDCA 网络在各种设置下都优于反向传播。损失景观中更新轨迹的可视化表明 TDCA 网络能够绕过基于 BP 的轨迹通常会陷入局部最小值的情况。 TDCA 网络在多任务优化方面也表现出色，在监督学习中的不同数据集和强化学习中的未见任务设置中展示了强大的通用性。此外，结果表明 TDCA 网络在跨不同架构训练神经网络方面具有广阔的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于生物大脑顶向下机制的深度神经网络训练框架，用以替代传统的反向传播算法。
AI.motivation: 反向传播算法虽广泛应用，但其生物学可行性存疑，亟需开发符合生物特性的替代训练方法。
AI.method: 设计双层训练框架，通过顶向下信用分配网络（TDCA-network）替代损失函数与反向传播，结合脑启发的信用扩散机制降低参数复杂度。
AI.result: 实验表明TDCA-network在非凸优化、监督学习及强化学习中均优于反向传播，能有效避开局部极小值并提升多任务泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: TDCA-network为生物可解释的深度网络训练提供了有效方案，适用于多种网络架构与任务场景。

authors_text: Chenyang Zhang,Xiyuan Wang,Chuyi Zhao,Yijing Ren,Tianyu Zhang,Zhenhui Peng,Xiaomeng Fan,Quan Li
title: PromotionLens: Inspecting Promotion Strategies of Online E-commerce via Visual Analytics
title_zh: PromotionLens：通过视觉分析检查在线电子商务的促销策略
comment: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics (Proc. IEEE VIS 2022)
comments: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics (Proc. IEEE VIS 2022)
abstract: Promotions are commonly used by e-commerce merchants to boost sales. The efficacy of different promotion strategies can help sellers adapt their offering to customer demand in order to survive and thrive. Current approaches to designing promotion strategies are either based on econometrics, which may not scale to large amounts of sales data, or are spontaneous and provide little explanation of sales volume. Moreover, accurately measuring the effects of promotion designs and making bootstrappable adjustments accordingly remains a challenge due to the incompleteness and complexity of the information describing promotion strategies and their market environments. We present PromotionLens, a visual analytics system for exploring, comparing, and modeling the impact of various promotion strategies. Our approach combines representative multivariant time-series forecasting models and well-designed visualizations to demonstrate and explain the impact of sales and promotional factors, and to support "what-if" analysis of promotions. Two case studies, expert feedback, and a qualitative user study demonstrate the efficacy of PromotionLens.

Current approaches to designing promotion strategies are either based on econometrics, which may not scale to large amounts of sales data, or are spontaneous and provide little explanation of sales volume. We present PromotionLens, a visual analytics system for exploring, comparing, and modeling the impact of various promotion strategies. Our approach combines representative multivariant time-series forecasting models and well-designed visualizations to demonstrate and explain the impact of sales and promotional factors, and to support "what-if" analysis of promotions. Two case studies, expert feedback, and a qualitative user study demonstrate the efficacy of PromotionLens.
abstract_zh: 电子商务商家通常使用促销来提高销量。不同促销策略的功效可以帮助卖家根据客户需求调整其产品，从而生存和发展。目前设计促销策略的方法要么基于计量经济学，可能无法扩展到大量销售数据，要么是自发的，几乎无法解释销售量。此外，由于描述促销策略及其市场环境的信息不完整且复杂，准确衡量促销设计的效果并相应地进行可引导的调整仍然是一个挑战。我们推出 PromotionLens，这是一个可视化分析系统，用于探索、比较和建模各种促销策略的影响。我们的方法结合了代表性的多变量时间序列预测模型和精心设计的可视化效果，以展示和解释销售和促销因素的影响，并支持促销的“假设”分析。两个案例研究、专家反馈和定性用户研究证明了 PromotionLens 的功效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PromotionLens系统，通过可视化分析方法帮助电商商家评估和优化促销策略。
AI.motivation: 现有促销策略分析方法（如计量经济学模型或自发式调整）存在可扩展性差、解释性不足等问题，难以应对大规模销售数据和复杂市场环境。
AI.method: 结合多变量时间序列预测模型与定制化可视化设计，构建支持销售影响分析和‘假设情景’模拟的交互式分析系统。
AI.result: 通过两个案例研究、专家反馈及用户实验验证，证明PromotionLens能有效揭示促销策略影响并辅助决策优化。
AI.conclusion: PromotionLens为电商促销策略分析提供了可扩展、可解释的可视化解决方案，有助于提升销售预测与策略调整效率。

search_text: PromotionLens: Inspecting Promotion Strategies of Online E-commerce via Visual Analytics PromotionLens：通过视觉分析检查在线电子商务的促销策略 Promotions are commonly used by e-commerce merchants to boost sales. The efficacy of different promotion strategies can help sellers adapt their offering to customer demand in order to survive and thrive. Current approaches to designing promotion strategies are either based on econometrics, which may not scale to large amounts of sales data, or are spontaneous and provide little explanation of sales volume. Moreover, accurately measuring the effects of promotion designs and making bootstrappable adjustments accordingly remains a challenge due to the incompleteness and complexity of the information describing promotion strategies and their market environments. We present PromotionLens, a visual analytics system for exploring, comparing, and modeling the impact of various promotion strategies. Our approach combines representative multivariant time-series forecasting models and well-designed visualizations to demonstrate and explain the impact of sales and promotional factors, and to support "what-if" analysis of promotions. Two case studies, expert feedback, and a qualitative user study demonstrate the efficacy of PromotionLens. 电子商务商家通常使用促销来提高销量。不同促销策略的功效可以帮助卖家根据客户需求调整其产品，从而生存和发展。目前设计促销策略的方法要么基于计量经济学，可能无法扩展到大量销售数据，要么是自发的，几乎无法解释销售量。此外，由于描述促销策略及其市场环境的信息不完整且复杂，准确衡量促销设计的效果并相应地进行可引导的调整仍然是一个挑战。我们推出 PromotionLens，这是一个可视化分析系统，用于探索、比较和建模各种促销策略的影响。我们的方法结合了代表性的多变量时间序列预测模型和精心设计的可视化效果，以展示和解释销售和促销因素的影响，并支持促销的“假设”分析。两个案例研究、专家反馈和定性用户研究证明了 PromotionLens 的功效。 本文提出PromotionLens系统，通过可视化分析方法帮助电商商家评估和优化促销策略。 Chenyang Zhang,Xiyuan Wang,Chuyi Zhao,Yijing Ren,Tianyu Zhang,Zhenhui Peng,Xiaomeng Fan,Quan Li cs.HC

authors_text: Minfeng Xu,Heng Guo,Jianfeng Zhang,Ke Yan,Le Lu
title: A New Probabilistic V-Net Model with Hierarchical Spatial Feature Transform for Efficient Abdominal Multi-Organ Segmentation
title_zh: 一种具有分层空间特征变换的新概率 V-Net 模型，用于高效腹部多器官分割
comment: 12 pages, 6 figures
comments: 12 pages, 6 figures
abstract: Accurate and robust abdominal multi-organ segmentation from CT imaging of different modalities is a challenging task due to complex inter- and intra-organ shape and appearance variations among abdominal organs. In this paper, we propose a probabilistic multi-organ segmentation network with hierarchical spatial-wise feature modulation to capture flexible organ semantic variants and inject the learnt variants into different scales of feature maps for guiding segmentation. More specifically, we design an input decomposition module via a conditional variational auto-encoder to learn organ-specific distributions on the low dimensional latent space and model richer organ semantic variations that is conditioned on input images.Then by integrating these learned variations into the V-Net decoder hierarchically via spatial feature transformation, which has the ability to convert the variations into conditional Affine transformation parameters for spatial-wise feature maps modulating and guiding the fine-scale segmentation. The proposed method is trained on the publicly available AbdomenCT-1K dataset and evaluated on two other open datasets, i.e., 100 challenging/pathological testing patient cases from AbdomenCT-1K fully-supervised abdominal organ segmentation benchmark and 90 cases from TCIA+&BTCV dataset. Highly competitive or superior quantitative segmentation results have been achieved using these datasets for four abdominal organs of liver, kidney, spleen and pancreas with reported Dice scores improved by 7.3% for kidneys and 9.7% for pancreas, while being ~7 times faster than two strong baseline segmentation methods(nnUNet and CoTr).
summary: Accurate and robust abdominal multi-organ segmentation from CT imaging of different modalities is a challenging task due to complex inter- and intra-organ shape and appearance variations among abdominal organs. In this paper, we propose a probabilistic multi-organ segmentation network with hierarchical spatial-wise feature modulation to capture flexible organ semantic variants and inject the learnt variants into different scales of feature maps for guiding segmentation. More specifically, we design an input decomposition module via a conditional variational auto-encoder to learn organ-specific distributions on the low dimensional latent space and model richer organ semantic variations that is conditioned on input images.Then by integrating these learned variations into the V-Net decoder hierarchically via spatial feature transformation, which has the ability to convert the variations into conditional Affine transformation parameters for spatial-wise feature maps modulating and guiding the fine-scale segmentation. The proposed method is trained on the publicly available AbdomenCT-1K dataset and evaluated on two other open datasets, i.e., 100 challenging/pathological testing patient cases from AbdomenCT-1K fully-supervised abdominal organ segmentation benchmark and 90 cases from TCIA+&BTCV dataset. Highly competitive or superior quantitative segmentation results have been achieved using these datasets for four abdominal organs of liver, kidney, spleen and pancreas with reported Dice scores improved by 7.3% for kidneys and 9.7% for pancreas, while being ~7 times faster than two strong baseline segmentation methods(nnUNet and CoTr).
abstract_zh: 由于腹部器官之间复杂的器官间和器官内形状以及外观变化，通过不同模式的 CT 成像进行准确而稳健的腹部多器官分割是一项具有挑战性的任务。在本文中，我们提出了一种具有分层空间特征调制的概率多器官分割网络，以捕获灵活的器官语义变体，并将学习到的变体注入不同尺度的特征图中以指导分割。更具体地说，我们通过条件变分自动编码器设计了一个输入分解模块，以学习低维潜在空间上的器官特定分布，并根据输入图像建模更丰富的器官语义变化。然后通过空间特征变换将这些学习到的变化分层集成到 V-Net 解码器中，该解码器能够将变化转换为条件仿射变换参数，用于空间特征图调制和指导精细分割。所提出的方法在公开的 AbdomenCT-1K 数据集上进行训练，并在另外两个开放数据集上进行评估，即来自 AbdomenCT-1K 完全监督的腹部器官分割基准的 100 个挑战性/病理测试患者病例和来自 TCIA+&BTCV 数据集的 90 个病例。使用这些数据集对肝脏、肾脏、脾脏和胰腺这四个腹部器官取得了极具竞争力或优异的定量分割结果，据报告，肾脏的 Dice 分数提高了 7.3%，胰腺的 Dice 分数提高了 9.7%，同时比两种强大的基线分割方法（nnUNet 和 CoTr）快了约 7 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于概率V-Net和分层空间特征变换的新型模型，用于提高腹部多器官CT图像分割的准确性和效率。
AI.motivation: 腹部器官因形状和外观的复杂变化导致分割困难，现有方法难以灵活捕捉器官语义变体并实现多尺度特征引导。
AI.method: 设计条件变分自编码器分解输入，学习低维潜在空间中的器官特异性分布，并通过空间特征变换将学习到的变体分层注入V-Net解码器，生成条件仿射变换参数以调制特征图。
AI.result: 在AbdomenCT-1K和TCIA+&BTCV数据集上，肾脏Dice分数提升7.3%，胰腺提升9.7%，分割速度较nnUNet和CoTr快7倍。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过分层空间特征调制有效捕捉器官语义变体，在保持高分割精度的同时显著提升计算效率，为医学影像分割提供新思路。

authors_text: Beibei Lin,Shunli Zhang,Ming Wang,Lincheng Li,Xin Yu
title: GaitGL: Learning Discriminative Global-Local Feature Representations for Gait Recognition
title_zh: GaitGL：学习用于步态识别的判别性全局局部特征表示
abstract: Existing gait recognition methods either directly establish Global Feature Representation (GFR) from original gait sequences or generate Local Feature Representation (LFR) from several local parts. However, GFR tends to neglect local details of human postures as the receptive fields become larger in the deeper network layers. Although LFR allows the network to focus on the detailed posture information of each local region, it neglects the relations among different local parts and thus only exploits limited local information of several specific regions. To solve these issues, we propose a global-local based gait recognition network, named GaitGL, to generate more discriminative feature representations. To be specific, a novel Global and Local Convolutional Layer (GLCL) is developed to take full advantage of both global visual information and local region details in each layer. GLCL is a dual-branch structure that consists of a GFR extractor and a mask-based LFR extractor. GFR extractor aims to extract contextual information, e.g., the relationship among various body parts, and the mask-based LFR extractor is presented to exploit the detailed posture changes of local regions. In addition, we introduce a novel mask-based strategy to improve the local feature extraction capability. Specifically, we design pairs of complementary masks to randomly occlude feature maps, and then train our mask-based LFR extractor on various occluded feature maps. In this manner, the LFR extractor will learn to fully exploit local information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GaitGL achieves better performance than state-of-the-art gait recognition methods. The average rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B, OU-MVLP, GREW and Gait3D is 93.6%, 98.7%, 68.0% and 63.8%, respectively, significantly outperforming the competing methods. The proposed method has won the first prize in two competitions: HID 2020 and HID 2021.
summary: Existing gait recognition methods either directly establish Global Feature Representation (GFR) from original gait sequences or generate Local Feature Representation (LFR) from several local parts. However, GFR tends to neglect local details of human postures as the receptive fields become larger in the deeper network layers. Although LFR allows the network to focus on the detailed posture information of each local region, it neglects the relations among different local parts and thus only exploits limited local information of several specific regions. To solve these issues, we propose a global-local based gait recognition network, named GaitGL, to generate more discriminative feature representations. To be specific, a novel Global and Local Convolutional Layer (GLCL) is developed to take full advantage of both global visual information and local region details in each layer. GLCL is a dual-branch structure that consists of a GFR extractor and a mask-based LFR extractor. GFR extractor aims to extract contextual information, e.g., the relationship among various body parts, and the mask-based LFR extractor is presented to exploit the detailed posture changes of local regions. In addition, we introduce a novel mask-based strategy to improve the local feature extraction capability. Specifically, we design pairs of complementary masks to randomly occlude feature maps, and then train our mask-based LFR extractor on various occluded feature maps. In this manner, the LFR extractor will learn to fully exploit local information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GaitGL achieves better performance than state-of-the-art gait recognition methods. The average rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B, OU-MVLP, GREW and Gait3D is 93.6%, 98.7%, 68.0% and 63.8%, respectively, significantly outperforming the competing methods. The proposed method has won the first prize in two competitions: HID 2020 and HID 2021.
abstract_zh: 现有的步态识别方法要么直接从原始步态序列建立全局特征表示（GFR），要么从多个局部部分生成局部特征表示（LFR）。然而，随着更深网络层的感受野变得更大，GFR 往往会忽略人体姿势的局部细节。尽管LFR允许网络关注每个局部区域的详细姿态信息，但它忽略了不同局部部分之间的关​​系，因此仅利用了几个特定区域的有限局部信息。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种基于全局-局部的步态识别网络，名为 GaitGL，以生成更具辨别力的特征表示。具体来说，开发了一种新颖的全局和局部卷积层（GLCL），以充分利用每层的全局视觉信息和局部区域细节。 GLCL是一种双分支结构，由GFR提取器和基于掩模的LFR提取器组成。 GFR 提取器旨在提取上下文信息，例如各个身体部位之间的关系，并且提出了基于掩模的 LFR 提取器来利用局部区域的详细姿势变化。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的基于掩模的策略来提高局部特征提取能力。具体来说，我们设计了成对的互补掩模来随机遮挡特征图，然后在各种遮挡特征图上训练基于掩模的 LFR 提取器。通过这种方式，LFR 提取器将学会充分利用本地信息。大量实验表明，GaitGL 比最先进的步态识别方法具有更好的性能。 CASIA-B、OU-MVLP、GREW 和 Gait3D 的平均排名 1 准确率分别为 93.6%、98.7%、68.0% 和 63.8%，显着优于竞争方法。所提出的方法在 HID 2020 和 HID 2021 两项竞赛中获得一等奖。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GaitGL方法，通过结合全局和局部特征表示提升步态识别性能。
AI.motivation: 现有步态识别方法要么仅关注全局特征而忽略局部细节，要么仅提取局部特征而忽视不同部位间的关联，导致特征表征能力受限。
AI.method: 设计双分支GLCL结构，包含全局特征提取器（捕捉身体部位关系）和基于掩码的局部特征提取器（通过随机遮挡训练增强局部细节学习能力）。
AI.result: 在CASIA-B、OU-MVLP等数据集上分别达到93.6%、98.7%的Rank-1准确率，优于现有方法并获HID 2020/2021竞赛冠军。
AI.conclusion: GaitGL通过有效融合全局与局部特征，显著提升了步态识别的判别能力，达到领域内先进水平。

search_text: GaitGL: Learning Discriminative Global-Local Feature Representations for Gait Recognition GaitGL：学习用于步态识别的判别性全局局部特征表示 Existing gait recognition methods either directly establish Global Feature Representation (GFR) from original gait sequences or generate Local Feature Representation (LFR) from several local parts. However, GFR tends to neglect local details of human postures as the receptive fields become larger in the deeper network layers. Although LFR allows the network to focus on the detailed posture information of each local region, it neglects the relations among different local parts and thus only exploits limited local information of several specific regions. To solve these issues, we propose a global-local based gait recognition network, named GaitGL, to generate more discriminative feature representations. To be specific, a novel Global and Local Convolutional Layer (GLCL) is developed to take full advantage of both global visual information and local region details in each layer. GLCL is a dual-branch structure that consists of a GFR extractor and a mask-based LFR extractor. GFR extractor aims to extract contextual information, e.g., the relationship among various body parts, and the mask-based LFR extractor is presented to exploit the detailed posture changes of local regions. In addition, we introduce a novel mask-based strategy to improve the local feature extraction capability. Specifically, we design pairs of complementary masks to randomly occlude feature maps, and then train our mask-based LFR extractor on various occluded feature maps. In this manner, the LFR extractor will learn to fully exploit local information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GaitGL achieves better performance than state-of-the-art gait recognition methods. The average rank-1 accuracy on CASIA-B, OU-MVLP, GREW and Gait3D is 93.6%, 98.7%, 68.0% and 63.8%, respectively, significantly outperforming the competing methods. The proposed method has won the first prize in two competitions: HID 2020 and HID 2021. 现有的步态识别方法要么直接从原始步态序列建立全局特征表示（GFR），要么从多个局部部分生成局部特征表示（LFR）。然而，随着更深网络层的感受野变得更大，GFR 往往会忽略人体姿势的局部细节。尽管LFR允许网络关注每个局部区域的详细姿态信息，但它忽略了不同局部部分之间的关​​系，因此仅利用了几个特定区域的有限局部信息。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种基于全局-局部的步态识别网络，名为 GaitGL，以生成更具辨别力的特征表示。具体来说，开发了一种新颖的全局和局部卷积层（GLCL），以充分利用每层的全局视觉信息和局部区域细节。 GLCL是一种双分支结构，由GFR提取器和基于掩模的LFR提取器组成。 GFR 提取器旨在提取上下文信息，例如各个身体部位之间的关系，并且提出了基于掩模的 LFR 提取器来利用局部区域的详细姿势变化。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的基于掩模的策略来提高局部特征提取能力。具体来说，我们设计了成对的互补掩模来随机遮挡特征图，然后在各种遮挡特征图上训练基于掩模的 LFR 提取器。通过这种方式，LFR 提取器将学会充分利用本地信息。大量实验表明，GaitGL 比最先进的步态识别方法具有更好的性能。 CASIA-B、OU-MVLP、GREW 和 Gait3D 的平均排名 1 准确率分别为 93.6%、98.7%、68.0% 和 63.8%，显着优于竞争方法。所提出的方法在 HID 2020 和 HID 2021 两项竞赛中获得一等奖。 本文提出GaitGL方法，通过结合全局和局部特征表示提升步态识别性能。 Beibei Lin,Shunli Zhang,Ming Wang,Lincheng Li,Xin Yu cs.CV

authors_text: Alex Bogatu,Zili Zhou,Dónal Landers,André Freitas
title: Active entailment encoding for explanation tree construction using parsimonious generation of hard negatives
title_zh: 使用简约生成硬否定的解释树构建的主动蕴涵编码
abstract: Entailment trees have been proposed to simulate the human reasoning process of explanation generation in the context of open--domain textual question answering. However, in practice, manually constructing these explanation trees proves a laborious process that requires active human involvement. Given the complexity of capturing the line of reasoning from question to the answer or from claim to premises, the issue arises of how to assist the user in efficiently constructing multi--level entailment trees given a large set of available facts. In this paper, we frame the construction of entailment trees as a sequence of active premise selection steps, i.e., for each intermediate node in an explanation tree, the expert needs to annotate positive and negative examples of premise facts from a large candidate list. We then iteratively fine--tune pre--trained Transformer models with the resulting positive and tightly controlled negative samples and aim to balance the encoding of semantic relationships and explanatory entailment relationships. Experimental evaluation confirms the measurable efficiency gains of the proposed active fine--tuning method in facilitating entailment trees construction: up to 20\% improvement in explanatory premise selection when compared against several alternatives.
summary: Entailment trees have been proposed to simulate the human reasoning process of explanation generation in the context of open--domain textual question answering. However, in practice, manually constructing these explanation trees proves a laborious process that requires active human involvement. Given the complexity of capturing the line of reasoning from question to the answer or from claim to premises, the issue arises of how to assist the user in efficiently constructing multi--level entailment trees given a large set of available facts. In this paper, we frame the construction of entailment trees as a sequence of active premise selection steps, i.e., for each intermediate node in an explanation tree, the expert needs to annotate positive and negative examples of premise facts from a large candidate list. We then iteratively fine--tune pre--trained Transformer models with the resulting positive and tightly controlled negative samples and aim to balance the encoding of semantic relationships and explanatory entailment relationships. Experimental evaluation confirms the measurable efficiency gains of the proposed active fine--tuning method in facilitating entailment trees construction: up to 20\% improvement in explanatory premise selection when compared against several alternatives.
abstract_zh: 蕴涵树被提出来模拟开放域文本问答背景下解释生成的人类推理过程。然而，在实践中，手动构建这些解释树被证明是一个费力的过程，需要人类的积极参与。考虑到捕获从问题到答案或从主张到前提的推理线的复杂性，出现的问题是如何帮助用户在给定大量可用事实的情况下有效地构建多级蕴涵树。在本文中，我们将蕴涵树的构造框架为一系列主动前提选择步骤，即，对于解释树中的每个中间节点，专家需要从大型候选列表中注释前提事实的正面和负面例子。然后，我们使用生成的正样本和严格控制的负样本迭代地微调预训练的 Transformer 模型，旨在平衡语义关系和解释蕴涵关系的编码。实验评估证实了所提出的主动微调方法在促进蕴涵树构建方面的可测量效率增益：与几种替代方法相比，解释性前提选择提高了 20%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过主动选择前提事实来高效构建多级蕴含树的方法，利用预训练Transformer模型的迭代微调。
AI.motivation: 手动构建蕴含树耗时且复杂，需开发自动化方法辅助用户从大量事实中高效选择解释性前提。
AI.method: 将蕴含树构建分解为多步骤前提选择任务，通过专家标注正负样本迭代微调模型，平衡语义关系与解释性蕴含关系的编码。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在解释性前提选择任务中相比其他方法效率提升20%。
AI.conclusion: 主动微调方法能有效平衡语义与逻辑关系建模，为大规模事实下的蕴含树构建提供可行解决方案。

authors_text: Syed Raza Bashir,Shaina Raza,Vojislav Misic
title: BERT4Loc: BERT for Location -- POI Recommender System
title_zh: BERT4Loc：定位 BERT——POI 推荐系统
abstract: Recommending points of interest (POIs) is a challenging task that requires extracting comprehensive location data from location-based social media platforms. To provide effective location-based recommendations, it's important to analyze users' historical behavior and preferences. In this study, we present a sophisticated location-aware recommendation system that uses Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) to offer personalized location-based suggestions. Our model combines location information and user preferences to provide more relevant recommendations compared to models that predict the next POI in a sequence. Our experiments on two benchmark dataset show that our BERT-based model outperforms various state-of-the-art sequential models. Moreover, we see the effectiveness of the proposed model for quality through additional experiments.
summary: Recommending points of interest (POIs) is a challenging task that requires extracting comprehensive location data from location-based social media platforms. To provide effective location-based recommendations, it's important to analyze users' historical behavior and preferences. In this study, we present a sophisticated location-aware recommendation system that uses Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) to offer personalized location-based suggestions. Our model combines location information and user preferences to provide more relevant recommendations compared to models that predict the next POI in a sequence. Our experiments on two benchmark dataset show that our BERT-based model outperforms various state-of-the-art sequential models. Moreover, we see the effectiveness of the proposed model for quality through additional experiments.
abstract_zh: 推荐兴趣点 (POI) 是一项具有挑战性的任务，需要从基于位置的社交媒体平台提取全面的位置数据。为了提供有效的基于位置的推荐，分析用户的历史行为和偏好非常重要。在这项研究中，我们提出了一个复杂的位置感知推荐系统，该系统使用 Transformers 的双向编码器表示 (BERT) 来提供基于位置的个性化建议。与预测序列中下一个 POI 的模型相比，我们的模型结合了位置信息和用户偏好，可提供更相关的推荐。我们对两个基准数据集的实验表明，我们基于 BERT 的模型优于各种最先进的序列模型。此外，我们通过额外的实验看到了所提出的质量模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于BERT的POI推荐系统，通过结合用户历史行为和位置信息提升推荐效果。
AI.motivation: 传统序列模型在POI推荐中难以全面捕捉用户偏好与位置上下文关系，需更有效的建模方法。
AI.method: 采用BERT模型编码用户行为序列与位置特征，融合用户偏好与空间信息进行个性化推荐。
AI.result: 在两个基准数据集上，BERT4Loc模型优于多种先进序列模型，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: BERT4Loc通过深度学习位置与用户行为关系，为POI推荐提供了更精准的解决方案。

ai_content.conclusion: BERT4Loc通过深度学习位置与用户行为关系，为POI推荐提供了更精准的解决方案。
